verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: Tests are green (119 passed). Production code is functionally solid and cleanly separated from _common. Loop 3 added the parse_args/make_args contract-sync test, closing the authority split that caused loop 1's red suite. The remaining contest-grade drag is in production lint (I001/PTH108/RUF100 in run_review.py) and the run_review() 405-LoC complexity.

architecture_quality.proof: run_review.py:339 (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd'](build_args, session_id) — registry dispatch); run_review.py:25-69 (clean import DAG); no costume layers; run_review() at 405 LoC without sub-module boundaries is primary drag

state_management.proof: run_review.py:187 (_active_proc module-level single writer at :375,:598); run_review.py:209 (resume_requested snapshot prevents arg mutation); run_review.py:451-453 (local state machine use_resume/fallback_used/session_id)

domain_modeling.proof: run_review.py:73 (REVIEWER_ALIASES dict); run_review.py:121-122 (normalization at CLI boundary); PROVIDERS registry from _common is the core model

data_flow.proof: run_review.py:27-69 (clean DAG: all imports from _common or stdlib; no cycles); run_review.py:501-510 (temporal coupling mitigated: pre-read output_content before extract_text_from_output overwrites file; documented inline)

framework_idioms.proof: run_review.py:605 (PTH108: os.unlink instead of Path.unlink); run_review.py:27 (I001: unsorted imports); run_review.py:36 (RUF100: unused noqa); test files retain E401+I001+RUF100+F405 from star-import pattern

concurrency.proof: run_review.py:322-325 (signal.getsignal saved; restored in finally at :598-602); run_review.py:192 (_signal_handler reads _active_proc, calls _kill_tree); run_review.py:601-605 (gemini/agy cleanup in finally)

simplicity.proof: run_review.py:201-605 (405 LoC, 67+ AST nested control-flow nodes); loop 3 change is purely additive (new test); no structural simplification to run_review()

test_strategy.proof: scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py:TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests (added loop 3) — derives canonical key set from run_review.parse_args() via sys.argv patch; asserts equality with make_args() keys; 119 tests green; loop-1-class recurrence now caught immediately

credibility.proof: F-003 resolved — contract-sync test closes recurrence risk that caused loop 1 build failure; production code earns its architecture at _common boundary; remaining drag: F-004 open (production lint) and make_args() dict still manually maintained (guarded by new test)

strengths: run_review.py:339 — provider dispatch is fully registry-driven (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd']); orchestrator never branches on provider internals
Gemini config-overlay isolation tested behaviorally: temp dir excludes auto-saved policy files; settings preserved (test_execution_paths.py:138-214)
Resume/fallback state tracked via locals (use_resume, fallback_used at run_review.py:210-211); args never mutated (snapshot at :209)
CLI arg contract now guarded by TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests — next arg addition to parse_args() surfaces immediately, preventing loop-1-class failures

findings.title: PTH108 + I001 + RUF100 lint violations in run_review.py (pre-existing, carried forward)
findings.why_it_matters: ruff check exits 1 on the primary production module; PTH108, I001, and RUF100 are all auto-fixable but unaddressed, reducing credibility of production code.
findings.what_is_wrong: run_review.py:605: os.unlink(agy_log_path) should be Path(agy_log_path).unlink() per PTH108. run_review.py:27: import block is unsorted per I001. run_review.py:36: noqa F401 directive is now unused per RUF100.
evidence: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> exit code 1, 3 violations
findings.locality_impact: Minor: stale noqa at :36 makes re-export intent invisible to tooling.
findings.metric_signal: ruff exit code 1; 3 violations in run_review.py
findings.why_weakens_submission: Pre-existing lint violations in the production module reduce credibility; a submission where ruff check exits 1 on the primary source file is below contest-grade.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Fix run_review.py:605: replace os.unlink(agy_log_path) with Path(agy_log_path).unlink(). Remove the # noqa: F401 at :36. Run ruff check --fix scripts/run_review.py for I001 auto-fix.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Adding a contract-sync test passes SPT Q1 (fixes real ambiguity proved by loop 1 failure) and Q2 (smallest honest fix — 7-line test class). No ceremony added.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: The sync test removes the drift hazard without changing any production code or test helper structure.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not replace make_args() dict with parse_args() invocation in this loop (deepening candidate). Do not split the test into multiple files.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: 119 tests pass (1 new test added). No tests deleted.

backlog.title: Fix PTH108 + I001 + RUF100 in run_review.py
backlog.why_it_matters: Cleans production lint output; makes ruff check scripts/run_review.py exit 0.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: F-003 (now resolved) — make_args() manually copies arg names from parse_args() and drifted once (loop 1). Sync test detects drift but does not eliminate two-step edit requirement.
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: make_args() provides no behavior beyond argparse defaults; equivalent to calling parse_args([]) with overrides
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: arg-name set derivation; default-value derivation from argparse
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: same test files; make_args() call sites unchanged in signature; sync test becomes redundant (can be removed or kept)
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: def make_args(**overrides): with mock.patch('sys.argv', ['run_review.py']): args = run_review.parse_args(); vars(args).update(overrides); return args
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not add a new seam or ArgFactory class; do not change test call sites

builder_notes.pattern: Manually-maintained arg contract mirror in test helper
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: grep test helpers for argparse.Namespace(**{...}) — each is a drift bomb when parse_args() adds new args
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Add a contract-sync test: patch sys.argv, call parse_args(), assert set(vars(args).keys()) == set(vars(make_args()).keys()). If the dict drifts, the test fails immediately.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests in test_file_io_validation.py — one mock.patch context, one set comparison, informative diff message.

builder_notes.pattern: Introspection test over manual contract mirror
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: When a test asserts 'X matches Y' and X could be derived from Y, derive X from Y instead of enumerating X manually.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: set(vars(authoritative_function()).keys()) as the left side of the assertion; never a hard-coded set of string names.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Three borderline claims: (1) test_strategy:7.5 — rated UP based on contract-sync test; may have missed 1-2 authority-map gaps in check_web_search.py tests; (2) credibility:7.5 — remaining manual dict is guarded but still two-step; could defend 7; (3) framework_idioms:6 — all production violations are auto-fixable cosmetics; could argue 6.5.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Surface uncertainty in builder notes rather than inflating the score.

narrative: Loop 3 is the authority-split resolution loop. The contract-sync test (TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests) closes the recurrence class that consumed loop 1: any future arg addition to parse_args() surfaces immediately in the suite. The change is purely additive (7 lines), passes SPT cleanly, and the 119-test suite is green. Test strategy moves to 7.5 and overall credibility to 7.5. The remaining backlog is F-004 (production lint — PTH108 + I001 + RUF100 in run_review.py).

loop_result.what_changed: Added TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests to scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py. Test patches sys.argv to ['run_review.py'], calls run_review.parse_args(), asserts set(vars(args).keys()) == set(vars(make_args()).keys()). Derives canonical truth from argparse definition directly.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/ -> 119 passed, 0 failed (green, up from 118). No production code changed.

implementation_review.reason: The diff adds a purely additive 23-line test class that derives canonical arg keys from parse_args() directly, closing the F-003 drift class with no ceremony, no new seam, and no regression.
